doc_lines: Full investment snapshot for one company — quote, financial health, analyst
Historical OHLCV bars for one or more market indices (default: major US indices).
Top US market movers — the biggest daily gainers or losers among tickers with
Options contracts for an underlying with current session pricing, filterable by
type, expiration range, and strike. US-listed options only — not for non-US
US stock-market sector performance — which sectors (Technology, Healthcare,
Energy, Financials, …) are up or down on the day. US only; not for non-US markets.
date: Analysis date "YYYY-MM-DD" (default: latest available). Historical
dates may be unavailable on the current data plan.
Historical daily OHLCV bars for one stock — for price history, trends, returns,
limit: Max records when no date range is given (default 60 trading days).
Screen/discover stocks by fundamental and market filters — market cap, price,
volume, beta, dividend, sector, industry, exchange, country, and type — via the
is_etf: ETFs only (True) or exclude ETFs (False).
is_fund: Funds only (True) or exclude funds (False).
is_actively_trading: Restrict to actively trading names.
Fetch historical dividend payments and stock splits — analyze dividend
growth, adjust prices for splits, or compare dividend history across peers.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type}. data is
{dividends, splits}, each a list; count is the total across both.
dividends fields: date, recordDate, paymentDate, declarationDate,
adjDividend, dividend, yield, frequency. splits fields: date, numerator,
denominator. Field names are FMP-native camelCase; date is "YYYY-MM-DD";
records are newest-first as returned by FMP. On error:
Fetch raw historical key metrics and financial ratios — track P/E, ROE,
and margins over time, or compare valuation across companies.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type, period}. data is
{key_metrics, ratios}, each a list of period records; count is the total.
key_metrics fields: date, marketCap, enterpriseValue, evToEBITDA,
freeCashFlowYield. ratios fields: date, netProfitMargin, returnOnEquity,
currentRatio, debtToEquityRatio, priceToEarningsRatio. Field names are
FMP-native camelCase; date is "YYYY-MM-DD"; records are newest-first as
returned by FMP. On error: {error: <code>, detail, symbol}.
Fetch raw historical financial statements for trend analysis or model
building. statement_type="all" returns income, balance sheet, and cash flow.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type, statement_type, period}.
One statement_type → a list of period records; "all" → {income_statement,
balance_sheet, cash_flow} lists with count the total. Common camelCase
fields: date, revenue, netIncome, eps, totalAssets, operatingCashFlow.
date is "YYYY-MM-DD"; records are newest-first as returned by FMP. On
Fetch raw historical growth rates for trend analysis — chart revenue/EPS
trajectory or compare growth across competitors.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type, period}. data is
{financial_growth, income_statement_growth}, each a list; count is the
total. financial_growth fields: date, revenueGrowth, netIncomeGrowth,
growthNetIncome, growthEPS. Growth values are decimal fractions
(0.1 = 10%). Field names are FMP-native camelCase; date is "YYYY-MM-DD";
records are newest-first as returned by FMP. On error:
Fetch DCF fair value and enterprise value history — track fair value vs
price or build valuation trend charts.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type, period}. data is
{current_dcf, historical_dcf, enterprise_value}, each a list; count is
the total. current_dcf fields: symbol, date, dcf, stockPrice.
historical_dcf is always [] — the stable FMP API no longer exposes it.
enterpriseValue. Field names are FMP-native camelCase; date is
"YYYY-MM-DD"; enterprise_value is newest-first as returned by FMP. On
Fetch insider trading transactions and aggregate buy/sell statistics —
detect insider buying clusters, screen unusual activity, or gauge C-suite
limit: Number of recent transactions to fetch (default 50).
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type}. data is {trades, stats},
each a list; count is the total across both. trades fields: symbol,
securitiesTransacted, price. stats fields: year, quarter, totalBought,
totalSold, buySellRatio. Field names are FMP-native camelCase; dates are
"YYYY-MM-DD"; trades are newest-first as returned by FMP. On error:
Fetch key executives with title and compensation — identify the
management team or compare executive pay across peers.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type}. data is a list of
executive records; count is the record total. Fields: name, title, pay,
currencyPay, gender, yearBorn, titleSince. Field names are FMP-native
camelCase; pay is an integer in currencyPay units; order is as returned
by FMP (not time-ordered). On error: {error: <code>, detail, symbol}.
Fetch shares float, outstanding shares, and float percentage — flag
low-float names, gauge ownership concentration, or screen squeeze candidates.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type}. data is a list of
records; count is the record total. Fields: symbol, date, freeFloat,
floatShares, outstandingShares. Field names are FMP-native camelCase;
date is "YYYY-MM-DD"; latest snapshot first as returned by FMP. On error:
Fetch a technical indicator time series over OHLCV bars — plot RSI,
overlay EMA/MACD, or screen by technical signals.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source, data_type, indicator, period,
timeframe}. data is a list of bars; count is the bar total. Fields: date,
open, high, low, close, volume, and the indicator value keyed by its
(intraday); field names are FMP-native camelCase; bars newest-first.
Fetch the earnings calendar for all companies reporting in a date range —
build a catalyst calendar, generate a morning note, or track earnings-season
dict: {count, data, source, data_type, from_date, to_date}. data is a
list of reporter records; count is the record total. Fields: symbol,
lastUpdated. actual fields are null before a company reports. Field names
are FMP-native camelCase; date is "YYYY-MM-DD"; order is as returned by
Fetch economic events with prior, estimate, and actual values — build a
catalyst calendar, generate a morning note, or track Fed meetings, jobs
to_date: End date "YYYY-MM-DD" (default: 7 days from today).
dict: {count, data, source, data_type, from_date, to_date}. data is a
list of events; count is the event total. Fields: date, country, event,
currency, previous, estimate, actual, change, changePercentage, impact,
unit. actual/estimate/previous are null for events not yet released.
Field names are FMP-native camelCase; date is "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS";
order is as returned by FMP. On error: {error: <code>, detail}.
Fetch an economic indicator time series — GDP growth for the macro
outlook, CPI/inflation for discount-rate assumptions, or unemployment and
the Fed funds rate for economic context.
limit: Number of observations to fetch (default 50).
dict: {count, data, source, data_type, indicator}. data is a list of
observations; count is the observation total. Fields: date, value (and
for some series, name). Field names are FMP-native camelCase; date is
"YYYY-MM-DD"; observations are newest-first as returned by FMP. On error:
Fetch market risk premium by country for CAPM/WACC — get the equity risk
premium for a DCF cost of equity, or compare premiums across markets.
dict: {count, data, source, data_type}. data is a list of country
records; count is the record total. Fields: country, continent,
(5.5 = 5.5%). Field names are FMP-native camelCase; order is by country
as returned by FMP (not time-ordered). On error: {error: <code>, detail}.
Fetch US Treasury rates across the full yield curve (1M to 30Y) — get the
risk-free rate for DCF/WACC (typically 10Y), read the curve shape, or track
dict: {count, data, source, data_type, from_date, to_date}. data is a
list of daily records; count is the record total. Each record has a date
plus per-tenor rate columns: month1, month2, month3, month6, year1,
year2, year3, year5, year7, year10, year20, year30. Rates are percent
(4.5 = 4.5%). Field names are FMP-native camelCase; date is "YYYY-MM-DD";
records are newest-first as returned by FMP. On error:
List options contracts for an underlying; use to discover/filter contracts.
Filter by call/put, expiration range, and strike range. ginlix-data only.
dict: {symbol, underlying_ticker, currency, timezone, count, data,
source}. data: list of contract dicts with ticker, contract_type,
strike_price in `currency` major units. On error: {error: <code>, detail}.
Fetch OHLCV bars for one options contract; use for option price history.
interval: Bar size; one of the intervals above.
dict: {symbol, interval, currency, timezone, count, data, source}. symbol
echoes the contract ticker; currency/timezone are the underlying's. data:
list of {date, open, high, low, close, volume} ascending (oldest first);
date exchange-local "YYYY-MM-DD[ HH:MM:SS]"; prices in `currency` major
Fetch real-time snapshots for options contracts; use for live quotes.
Session OHLCV is always present; bid/ask and last trade populate during
options_tickers: One or more OCC tickers, comma-separated
dict: {currency, timezone, count, data, source}. currency/timezone are
the underlying's, set only when all contracts share one (else omitted).
data: list of snapshot dicts, each with ticker, name, market_status,
(price/size); prices in `currency` major units. On error:
Fetch OHLCV bars for a stock, commodity, crypto, or forex pair.
interval: Bar size; see intervals above.
from_date: "YYYY-MM-DD" (append " HH:MM" for intraday time filtering).
to_date: "YYYY-MM-DD" (append " HH:MM" for intraday time filtering).
dict: {symbol, asset_type, interval, currency, timezone, count, data,
source}. data: list of {date, open, high, low, close, volume} ascending
(oldest first); date exchange-local "YYYY-MM-DD[ HH:MM:SS]"; prices in
Fetch short interest and/or short volume for a US stock (short-squeeze work).
Short interest is FINRA bi-monthly (settlement_date); short volume is daily
limit: Max records per section (default 20, max 50000).
dict: {symbol, timezone, count, data, source, data_type}. data is a dict
of {short_interest, short_volume} lists (requested sections only), newest
first. Record fields are vendor-native (e.g. settlement_date,
short_volume_ratio). Partial failures appear in an `errors` map. On
Fetch OHLCV price bars for a stock; use for historical charts/analysis.
accepted). start_date and end_date are required for intraday.
symbol: Ticker — US "AAPL", HK "0700.HK", A-share "600519.SS", LSE "VOD.L".
interval: Bar size; one of the intervals above.
start_date: "YYYY-MM-DD" (append " HH:MM" for intraday time filtering).
end_date: "YYYY-MM-DD" (append " HH:MM" for intraday time filtering).
dict: {symbol, interval, currency, timezone, count, data, source}. data:
list of {date, open, high, low, close, volume} ascending (oldest first);
(intraday); prices in `currency` major units. On error:
Analyst price-target summary for a stock. Use for consensus target vs.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a single Yahoo-native
record {current, low, high, mean, median}; price values are as reported
by Yahoo (minor units on some venues, e.g. LSE pence) and currency is
omitted — no declared-currency source on this path. count is 1 when data
exists, else 0 (empty {} when unavailable). On error:
Aggregated analyst recommendation counts by period. Use to gauge the
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
records {period, strongbuy, buy, hold, sell, strongsell} in Yahoo's
order (period "0m" current, "-1m", "-2m", "-3m"). Empty data means none
published. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with error upstream_error.
Forward EPS estimates by period. Use for consensus EPS for coming
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
avg, low, high, yearagoeps, growth} in Yahoo's period order. Empty data
means none published. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with error
Historical EPS estimate vs. actual with surprise. Use to review recent
earnings beats/misses. Same data as get_earnings_data (fundamentals server).
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
in Yahoo's order (oldest first); quarter is "YYYY-MM-DD". Empty data
means none reported. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with error
Growth estimates for the stock vs. industry, sector, and index. Use to
compare a name's growth outlook to its peers.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
industry, sector, index}, each a growth rate, in Yahoo's period order.
Empty data means none published. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with
Current insiders and their holdings. Use to see who the insiders are and
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
shares_owned_indirectly, ...} in Yahoo's order; date fields are
"YYYY-MM-DD". Empty data means none reported. On error:
Recent insider buy/sell transactions. Use to track insider activity.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
records {start_date, insider, position, url, transaction, text, shares,
value, ownership} in Yahoo's order (most recent first); start_date is
"YYYY-MM-DD". Empty data means none reported. On error:
Top institutional holders of a stock. Use to see which institutions hold
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
records {date_reported, holder, shares, value, pctheld} in Yahoo's order
(largest holders first); date_reported is "YYYY-MM-DD". Empty data means
none reported. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with error
Ownership breakdown (insider %, institution %, etc.). Use for a
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
records {breakdown, value} where breakdown is the label and value the
percentage or count. Empty data means unavailable. On error:
Top mutual-fund holders of a stock. Use to see the largest fund
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
records {date_reported, holder, shares, value, pctheld} in Yahoo's order
(largest first); date_reported is "YYYY-MM-DD". Empty data means none
reported. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with error upstream_error.
Latest news articles for a stock. Use to pull recent headlines and
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of raw Yahoo-native
article dicts in Yahoo's order (most recent first); each has nested
`content` with title, publisher, url, and publish-date fields (exact
shape is Yahoo-native and varies). Empty data means no articles. On
error: {error, detail, symbol} with error upstream_error.
Forward revenue estimates by period. Use for consensus revenue for coming
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
avg, low, high, yearagorevenue, growth} in Yahoo's period order. Empty
data means none published. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with error
ESG / sustainability scores for a company. Use for ESG risk context.
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a single Yahoo-native ESG
record mapping metric name → value (e.g. totalEsg, environmentScore,
socialScore, governanceScore); missing metrics are null. count is 1 when
data exists, else 0 (empty {} when Yahoo has no ESG coverage). On error:
History of analyst rating changes (upgrades/downgrades). Use to track
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
records {gradedate, firm, tograde, fromgrade, action} in Yahoo's order
(most recent first); gradedate is "YYYY-MM-DD". Empty data means none
reported. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with error upstream_error.
Financial statements for several companies for side-by-side comparison.
quarterly: Quarterly if True, else annual.
successful_tickers, errors?}. data is keyed by canonical symbol →
first, reporting-currency native units, Yahoo-native metric names); count
is total metrics across symbols. Failed/empty symbols are omitted and
listed in errors as {error, detail, symbol}. On a bad statement_type:
Valuation multiples across several stocks for side-by-side comparison.
dict: {count, data, source, successful_tickers, errors?}. data is keyed
by canonical symbol → a dict of snake_case metrics: trailing_p_e,
current_price (nulls where absent); count is the number of symbols
returned. Failed symbols are omitted and listed in errors as
Balance sheet (assets, liabilities, equity). Use for leverage, liquidity,
quarterly: Quarterly if True, else annual.
dict: {symbol, quarterly, count, data, source}. data is Yahoo-native
"Total Debt"); date keys "YYYY-MM-DD" ordered newest first; values in the
company's reporting currency (native units). count is the number of
metrics. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with error
Cash flow statement (operating, investing, financing). Use for cash
quarterly: Quarterly if True, else annual.
dict: {symbol, quarterly, count, data, source}. data is Yahoo-native
ordered newest first; values in the company's reporting currency (native
units). count is the number of metrics. On error:
Company profile and key statistics. Use for sector/industry, market cap,
dict: {symbol, currency?, count, data, source}. data is one Yahoo-native
record — a flat dict of Yahoo `info` fields (e.g. shortName, sector,
longBusinessSummary); field names are Yahoo-native, price-scale fields
are unconverted, and None values are dropped. currency is Yahoo's
declared trading currency (a minor-unit label like GBp on some venues),
omitted when Yahoo declares none; count is 1. On error:
Historical EPS estimate vs. actual per quarter. Use to review earnings
beats/misses. Same data as get_earnings_history (analysis server).
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
in Yahoo's order (oldest first); quarter is "YYYY-MM-DD". On error:
Earnings announcement dates with EPS estimate vs. actual. Use for the
dict: {symbol, count, data, source}. data is a list of Yahoo-native
newest first (future dates included, with null reported_eps/surprise_pct);
earnings_date is exchange-local "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS". On error:
Income statement (revenue, expenses, margins, net income). Use for
quarterly: Quarterly if True, else annual.
dict: {symbol, quarterly, count, data, source}. data is Yahoo-native
"Total Revenue", "Net Income"); date keys are "YYYY-MM-DD" ordered newest
first; values are in the company's reporting currency (native units).
count is the number of metrics. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with
Historical earnings (EPS estimate vs. actual) for several stocks at once.
dict: {count, data, source, successful_tickers, errors?}. data is keyed
by canonical symbol → {count, earnings}; each earnings is a list of
surprisepercent} in Yahoo's order (oldest first), quarter "YYYY-MM-DD".
Top-level count is total earnings records across symbols. Failed/empty
symbols are omitted and listed in errors as {error, detail, symbol}.
Earnings announcements scheduled within a date range. Use to find which
dict: {start, end, count, data, source}. data is a list of records
reported_eps, surprise_pct}, in Yahoo's native order; event_start_date
is "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" exchange-local. Field names are Yahoo-native.
On error: {error, detail} with error upstream_error.
Overview of an industry — top performing and top growth companies plus
its parent sector. Use for industry-level context and constituents.
dict: {industry, count, data, source}. data is {overview,
sector_name}: overview is a Yahoo-native dict, the two company lists are
growth_estimate). count is top_performing + top_growth. On error:
Current status and index summary for a market. Use to check whether a
market is open and read headline index moves.
Markets: US, GB, ASIA, EUROPE, RATES, COMMODITIES, CURRENCIES,
dict: {market, count, data, source}. data is {status, summary}, both
Yahoo-native: status holds open/close times and timezone fields,
summary is keyed by exchange/index with price and change fields. count
is 1 (one status snapshot). On error: {error, detail} with error
Run a named Yahoo predefined screener. Use for common ready-made scans
returns the full list of valid names in `supported`.
screen_name: A Yahoo predefined screener name (see above).
dict: {screen_name, count, data, source}. data is the Yahoo-native
screen result {quotes: [...], ...}; quotes are raw Yahoo quote dicts,
count is the number returned. On an unknown name:
{error: invalid_argument, detail, supported}. On upstream failure:
Overview of a market sector — top companies, ETFs, and its industries.
Use for sector-level context and constituents.
Sector keys: technology, healthcare, financial-services, consumer-cyclical,
sector_key: One of the keys above.
dict: {sector, count, data, source}. data is {overview, top_companies,
top_etfs, industries}: overview and top_etfs are Yahoo-native dicts,
top_companies and industries are lists of Yahoo-native records. count is
top_companies + industries. On error: {error, detail} with error
Screen equities with custom Yahoo filters — find stocks matching
Each filter is {operator, operands}; operators: gt, lt, gte, lte, eq, btwn,
or nested with and/or. Multiple top-level filters are auto-wrapped in AND.
filters: List of filter dicts (see above).
sort_asc: Ascending if True (default False = descending).
dict: {count, data, source}. data is the Yahoo-native screen result
{quotes: [...], ...}; quotes are raw Yahoo quote dicts ordered by
sort_field, count is the number returned. On error: {error, detail} —
Look up tickers and related news by free-text keyword. Use to resolve a
company name to symbols or scan headlines.
query: Keyword — company name or symbol, e.g. "apple", "AAPL".
max_results: Max ticker quotes to return (default 8).
news_count: Max news articles to return (default 5).
dict: {count, data, source}. data is {quotes: [...], news: [...]}, both
Yahoo-native and in Yahoo's relevance order; quote dicts carry a
`symbol` key plus name/exchange fields, news dicts are raw Yahoo article
records. count is quotes + news. On error: {error, detail} with error
Full dividend and stock-split history for one ticker. Use for
dict: {symbol, currency, timezone, count, data, source,
(oldest first); date is exchange-local "YYYY-MM-DD", amounts in
`currency` major units (minor-unit quotes like GBp are converted, e.g.
pence→pounds), ratio is the split factor (e.g. 4.0 = 4-for-1);
count is dividends + splits. On error: {error, detail, symbol} with
Dividend history for several stocks in one call. Use to compare payout
dict: {count, data, source, errors?}. data is keyed by canonical symbol
→ {count, currency?, dividends}; each dividends is a list of
"YYYY-MM-DD", amounts in that symbol's major currency units (minor-unit
quotes like GBp are converted, e.g. pence→pounds); count is the total
dividends across symbols. Failed symbols are omitted from data and listed
Historical OHLCV bars for several stocks — compare price series.
dict: {interval, count, data, source, period, errors?}. data is keyed by
canonical symbol → {count, currency?, timezone?, data}; each inner data
is a list of {date, open, high, low, close, volume, dividends?, splits?}
is total bars. Prices in major units (GBp/pence auto-converted).
Failed symbols are listed in errors as
{error, detail, symbol}; a bad interval returns
Historical OHLCV price bars for one stock — charts, returns, technicals.
dict: {symbol, interval, period, currency, timezone, count, data,
source}. data is a list of {date, open, high, low, close, volume,
ascending (oldest first); date is exchange-local "YYYY-MM-DD" or
"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" intraday, timezone is the IANA zone, prices in
`currency` major units (minor-unit GBp/pence auto-converted to major).
